records.ety: [D. boei buoy, fetter, fr. OF. boie, buie, chain, fetter, F. boue a buoy, from L. boia. “Boiae genus vinculorum tam ferreae quam ligneae.” Festus. So called because chained to its place.]

defs: A float; esp. a floating object moored to the bottom, to mark a channel or to point out the position of something beneath the water, as an anchor, shoal, rock, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Buoyed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Buoying.]

defs: To keep from sinking in a fluid, as in water or air; to keep afloat; -- with up.
To support or sustain; to preserve from sinking into ruin or despondency.
quotes: Those old prejudices, which buoy up the ponderous mass of his nobility, wealth, and title.

defs: To fix buoys to; to mark by a buoy or by buoys; as, to buoy an anchor; to buoy or buoy off a channel.
quotes: Not one rock near the surface was discovered which was not buoyed by this floating weed.

defs: To float; to rise like a buoy.
